title: Bank of America Statement Analysis for Mortgage and Lending
meta: Bank of America is one of the most common banks in loan applications. Learn how to read BofA statement formats and extract key data for lending decisions.
content:
Bank of America Statement Format

Bank of America is one of the largest banks in the United States, and their bank statements are among the most frequently reviewed in mortgage and business lending. Understanding their specific format helps analysts work more efficiently.

BofA Statement Structure

  • Account summary box: Period, beginning balance, deposits/credits, withdrawals/debits, and ending balance prominently displayed at the top
  • Deposits and Credits section: Chronological listing of all incoming transactions
  • Withdrawals and Debits section: All outgoing transactions
  • Checks section: Any checks written during the period
  • Service Fees section: All fees charged

BofA Direct Deposit Identification

Bank of America direct deposits typically show as 'DES:DIRECT DEP' entries with the employer name in the description field. Business deposits appear as 'ACH CREDIT' with the payer's name.

BofA Zelle and Digital Payments

Bank of America integrates Zelle, so Zelle payments appear directly in the transaction history as 'ZELLE PAYMENT FROM [NAME]' for received payments. These may or may not qualify as income depending on their regularity and source.

BofA SafeBalance Account Considerations

BofA's SafeBalance account has no overdraft — transactions are simply declined when funds are insufficient. This means no overdraft fees, but frequent declines may still indicate cash flow issues. These declines don't always appear in statements, so a SafeBalance account with very low balances is still concerning even without NSF fees.

Analyzing BofA Business Accounts

BofA Business Advantage accounts have similar structure to personal accounts but may include additional sections for business-specific features like payroll processing or merchant services. StatementScrub handles all BofA account types and formats automatically.
